messages.subject: [R-sig-DCM] Welcome!
messages.body_plain:
Welcome to the R-SIG-DCM list.  This is just a short message to say hi, 
and to test that the system is actually working.  (After having existed 
for about 24 hours and having less than a dozen members, already a 
possible problem has emerged.)

None of the list owners have had experience with Mailman management, so 
please bear with us if things seem a bit flakey.  I am keen to help if 
you do experience problems, so don't be shy in letting me know.

Cheers,

John


-- 
Department of Marketing
University of Otago

http://marketing.otago.ac.nz/staff/williamsj.asp

messages.subject: [R-sig-DCM] Welcome! [Sec: UNOFFICIAL]
messages.body_plain:
I found the process to join this group to be as quick as the process to join the R mail list.

I'm looking forward to seeing the discussions develop in this list. :)

Cheers
Michelle

-----Original Message-----
From: r-sig-dcm-bounces at r-project.org [mailto:r-sig-dcm-bounces at r-project.org] On Behalf Of John Williams
Sent: Wednesday, 14 July 2010 8:31 AM
To: r-sig-dcm at r-project.org
Subject: [R-sig-DCM] Welcome!

Welcome to the R-SIG-DCM list.  This is just a short message to say hi, 
and to test that the system is actually working.  (After having existed 
for about 24 hours and having less than a dozen members, already a 
possible problem has emerged.)

None of the list owners have had experience with Mailman management, so 
please bear with us if things seem a bit flakey.  I am keen to help if 
you do experience problems, so don't be shy in letting me know.

Cheers,

John

University of Otago

messages.subject: [R-sig-DCM] a  discrete choice dynamic model with panel data
messages.subject_clean: a  discrete choice dynamic model with panel data
messages.body_plain:
Hey, all

I just visited this forum but it seems few people here. I am totally new to R 
and 

some kind new to the dynamic mode. any suggestion are appreciated.

I am modeling the labour market choices, such as un/employment, schooling? etc. 
people choose one of them in each period to maximize their present value of life 
time utility.

here is my model:
Vijt = X_ij*beta + ipsilon_ijt
ipsilon_ijt = mu_j + sigma_t

where?i means individual, j the decision/choice and t the period.
thus Vijt?is the utility individual i can get from choice j at time t.
X are know vector of variables with parameter vector beta

ipsilon is the residual which consist of a well behaved sigma and 
unkonw hetrogeneity among individuals mu, which have the form
mu_j = alpa_j + gama_j*eta

eta is individual types. that is, the intercept and slope of the laten 
variable mu both depend on the his/er choice. the model will be
Vijt = X_ij*beta + alpa_j + gama_j*eta + sigma_t

alpa, beta, gama, plus eta,?are parameters that need to be estimated.

the model will be estimated with simulated maximum likelihood method
with probability for individual i and choice j at peroid t. 

PRijt?= exp(Vijt)/(exp(Vi1t)+ exp(Vi2t)+ ....+ exp(ViJt))

if I write?a function to represent the upper maximu likelihood for all i at each 
t and every choice j,?can the command optim be used to get the estimates? any 
suggestions are apprecited


Nan
